mission.title: knative: volume mount break Pod startup
mission.description: volume mount break Pod startup. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify knative troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your knative deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative -n knative --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review knative configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get configmap -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative
```
## In what area(s)?

## What version of Knative?

HEAD

## Expected Behavior

Run the `nginx` container as a service by specifying a non default port (.e port 80).

steps.title: Apply the fix for volume mount break Pod startup
steps.description:
Now (#4156) that there is no fluentd sidecar we could also get rid of the emptyDir.
 &  mentionned that we should be able to read directly from the container content.

If there is no volume mounted to `/var/log`, its content is located at `/var/lib/docker/overlay2/<ID>/merged/var/log`. We could
```yaml
2019-04-18T16:41:52.643263735Z nginx: [alert] could not open error log file: open() "/var/log/nginx/error.log" failed (2: No such file or directory)
2019-04-18T16:41:52.649571891Z 2019/04/18 16:41:52 [emerg] 1#1: open() "/var/log/nginx/error.log" failed (2: No such file or directory)
```

steps.title: Confirm volume mount break Pod startup is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative -n knative --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n knative --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Now (#4156) that there is no fluentd sidecar we could also get rid of the emptyDir.
 &  mentionned that we should be able to read directly from the container content.

If there is no volume mounted to `/var/log`, its content is located at `/var/lib/docker/overlay2/<ID>/merged/var/log`.
codeSnippets:
2019-04-18T16:41:52.643263735Z nginx: [alert] could not open error log file: open() "/var/log/nginx/error.log" failed (2: No such file or directory)
2019-04-18T16:41:52.649571891Z 2019/04/18 16:41:52 [emerg] 1#1: open() "/var/log/nginx/error.log" failed (2: No such file or directory)

apiVersion: serving.knative.dev/v1alpha1
kind: Service
metadata:
    annotations:
        serving.knative.dev/creator: 'system:serviceaccount:sebgoa:default'
        serving.knative.dev/lastModifier: 'system:serviceaccount:sebgoa:default'
    name: fooport
spec:
    runLatest:
        configuration:
            revisionTemplate: {metadata: {creationTimestamp: null}, spec: {container: {image: nginx, name: "", ports: [{containerPort: 80}], resources: {requests: {cpu: 400m}}}, timeoutSeconds: 300}}

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative installed or the issue environment reproducible.
